hook.text: The Sun blasts charged particles at 1.6 million km/h in every direction. That stream is the solar wind.

buildup.text: It carries protons and electrons past every planet, reaching far beyond Pluto into deep space.

discovery.text: Earth's magnetic field deflects most of the solar wind. Without it, our atmosphere would be stripped away.

twist.text: Mars lost its magnetic field 4 billion years ago. The solar wind stripped its atmosphere, leaving it barren.

climax.text: The heliopause is where the solar wind meets interstellar space. Voyager 1 crossed it in 2012.

punchline.text: The Sun exhales. Our magnetic field is the umbrella.

quiz.question: Why did Mars lose most of its atmosphere?
options: Its magnetic field died, and the solar wind stripped the atmosphere away
A giant asteroid impact blew the atmosphere into space
Mars was always too small to hold any atmosphere
